Exclusive: Chelsea leading Higuain chase, Juve identify Icardi as replacement

Juventus could allow Gonzalo Higuain leave the club, if they receive a right offer this summer and replace him with Inter’s Mauro Icardi, Caliomercato.com understands.

​Gonzalo Higuain arrived in Turin for a fee of €90 million from Napoli two years ago. The goals scored by him are relatively to little less for the investment Juvenus made. The Argentine can now start and is set to move more and more away from Juventus.

Chelsea are already leading the race for Higuain and Juventus believe they can get around €70 million. This will be a good news for Chelsea, especially if they appoint Maurizio Sarri as their new manager. PSG are also interested in Higuain, but Juventus will only allow him leave if they find a replacement.

​Juventus have already made numerous contacts with Icardi’s agent and wife Wanda Nara. The talks have been positive and now have to convince Inter to allow him leave this summer. His release clause €100 million is only for foreign clubs, but there are ways to work around it.

https://www.calciomercato.com/en/news/chelsea-leading-e70m-rated-star-chase-club-identifies-icardi-as--69106

Higuain and €50m for Icardi?
By Football Italia staff

The Corriere della Sera claims Juventus have offered Gonzalo Higuain plus €50m for Mauro Icardi, with Inter prepared to listen.

The rumours of Juve trying to snatch the Capocannoniere away to Turin have been circulating for a few days now.

Icardi has a release clause worth €110m, but it is valid only from July 1-20 and to clubs outside of Italy.

According to the Corriere della Sera newspaper, Juve’s lawyers are studying the Inter contract to see if there’s a way they can activate the clause regardless.

If not, the offer on the table is €50m cash plus Higuain, who they value at €60m, taking the total up to €110m.

The real surprise in this story is that Inter and Icardi are prepared to listen.

Icardi’s wife and representative, Wanda Nara, said last week that they will either work out a new contract with Inter or move on.

While Higuain is going to the 2018 World Cup with Argentina, Icardi has been left behind, despite scoring 29 Serie A goals in 34 games this season.

Higuain, who is five years older than Icardi, scored 23 in 50 competitive matches for Juve.

香港時間6月1日淩晨《紐約時報》消息，米蘭已被歐洲足協禁止參加新賽季的歐霸盃，原因是違反財政公平法案。

　　歐洲足協對於米蘭的財政問題一直高度關注，前不久他們剛剛否決了米蘭提出的財政自願和解協議。　

　　李勇鴻通過美國洛迪高伊利奧特對衝基金的3億歐元貸款完成了對AC米蘭球會的收購，這筆高息貸款將在10月份償還。

　　李勇鴻面臨的全額還款數字高達3.8億歐元。據《紐約時報》透露，兩名參與貸款過程的工作人員確認，歐洲足協已經決定將米蘭排除出新賽季的歐霸盃比賽。

　　歐洲足協目前還有發佈官方消息，不過《紐約時報》認為此事變數不大。如果米蘭被排除出歐霸盃，阿特蘭大將獲得直接參加小組賽的資格，費倫天拿則將可以參加歐霸盃資格賽。

Arias: 'Juve would be a dream'
By Football Italia staff

PSV Eindhoven right-back Santiago Arias denies he had a Juventus medical, but does admit the Bianconeri “would be a dream.”

There had been reports last month that the 26-year-old was on the verge of a transfer to Turin.

“I read that too, but to be honest, when it was written that I was in Turin, I was still in Holland,” he told La Gazzetta dello Sport after Colombia’s friendly game against Egypt.

“I don’t know what will happen with my future, as I have no news right now. Of course, it’d be a dream to play for Juve…”

Bianconeri director of sport Fabio Paratici was in the stands to view that Colombia match and is ready to go for Arias as a replacement for Stephan Lichtsteiner, whose contract has run down.

Matteo Darmian had been the favourite, but Juventus have no intention of going above the €12m plus bonuses offer and Manchester United are holding out for €20m.

Juventus have agreed terms for the transfer of Mattia Perin and attention has now turned to Valencia full-back Joao Cancelo.

Sky Sport Italia’s Gianluca Di Marzio reports that the Bianconeri have agreed terms with Genoa on a €15m deal. Juve will initially pay €12m and up to €3m in bonuses for Perin who’ll a four-year contract worth €2.5m a season.

The 25-year-old keeper is also expected in Turin on Friday for a medical having made close to 200 appearances for Genoa during a seven year spell.

With Emre Can’s deal also seemingly complete, Juventus have turned their attention to the full-back position and are looking at Joao Cancelo as a possible replacement for Stephan Lichtsteiner who recently joined Arsenal.

Di Marzio reports that Cancelo has rejected Wolverhampton Wanderers and would prefer to join a ‘a bigger club’ than Wolves, despite them meeting Valencia’s €40m valuation of the player.

Juve are prepared to offer the Portuguese full-back a season-long loan with an obligation to buy outright at the end of the campaign.

Reports from Italy suggest Joao Cancelo has made it clear to his agent Jorge Mendes that he wants to join Juventus this summer.

The Portuguese right-back spent an impressive season on loan at Inter Milan and the Bianconeri are now seriously interested in bringing him to Turin, despite a failed bid last summer.

Corriere dello Sport report that Cancelo has informed his agent of his desire to join Juventus having turned down the opportunity to play in the Premier League with Wolves.

Juve have the agreement of the player but will still need to work out a deal with Valencia who are asking €40m for the talented youngster.

It’s reported the Bianconeri will offer to take the player on loan for a figure of around €5-6m with redemption obligation set at between €25-30m.

Talks are set to continue between the two clubs however Juve are also following Matteo Darmian, despite being at loggerheads with Manchester United over their valuation of the player.

Milinkovic Savic's father: "Juventus would be the ideal team for him"

"Juventus would be the ideal, perfect team for him. In Turin, he'd have the possibility to grow and improve to get to his best level in a prestigious and winning club which has dominated the league for many years". Sergej Milinkovic Savic hasn't any doubts about his son's future. "After three seasons in Rome, Sergej knows the Italian league perfectly, he adapted very well and has learned to speak Italian" has added Nikola Milinkovic-Savic to Tuttosport.

"With players of such a great level like Dybala, Douglas Costa and the others, he could win the Scudetto, the Coppa Italia and help to win the Champions League, why not? Juventus always go far in this competition. If he were to win the most prestigious European cup, Sergej could also have the ambition to win the Ballon d'Or. You can only be among the candidates by playing in one of the top European teams...".

There also are rumors about the possibility to go in Spain: "He could play in La Liga after his 25th birthday, as I said it would be better for him to get to his best level in Italy first. Exactly like Zidane did".
